This psalm provides helpful thoughts for difficult times; you may comfort yourself with such meditations and prayers. Let us see what makes the times bad, and when they may be considered so. Ask people of this world, What makes the times bad? They will tell you that a scarcity of money, a decline in trade, and the devastation of war make the times bad: but Scripture attributes the badness of the times to causes of another nature, &c.: perilous times will come because sin will abound; and David complains about this. When piety decays, times are truly bad. He who made man’s mouth will call him to account for his proud, profane, dissembling, or even useless words. When the poor and needy are oppressed, then the times are very bad. God himself takes notice of the oppression of the poor and the sighing of the needy. When wickedness abounds and is supported by those in authority, then the times are very bad. See with what good things we are here provided for such difficult times; and we cannot know what times we may be kept alive for. 1. We have a God to go to, from whom you may ask and expect relief from all your troubles. 2. God will certainly punish and restrain false and proud men. 3. God will work deliverance for his oppressed people. His help comes at the most appropriate time. Though people are unreliable, God is faithful; though they cannot be trusted, God can be. The preciousness of God’s word is compared to silver refined to the highest degree. How many proofs have been given of its power and truth! God will protect his chosen remnant, no matter how bad the times are. As long as the world exists, there will be a generation of proud and wicked men. But all of God's people are entrusted to Christ our Savior; they are safe there, because no one can take them away; being built on Him, the Rock, they are secure, even if temptation or persecution comes with great force upon them.
